title: Self Driving Products: Product Signals to Pull Requests — Joshua Snyder, PostHog
summary: PostHog is building an autonomous system that ingests product signals like errors and user interactions, uses a background agent to group related issues and analyze code, then automatically opens pull requests with fixes. The system leverages embeddings, LLM-generated queries, and iterative agent execution to improve specificity and reduce expensive inference steps.
writeup: PostHog's Joshua Snyder details an autonomous system that turns product signals, including errors and user interactions, into pull requests. A background agent groups related issues, analyzes the relevant code, and opens PRs with proposed fixes. The pipeline combines embeddings, LLM-generated queries, and iterative agent execution, with the design aimed at improving specificity and cutting down on expensive inference steps. The talk maps out an agentic workflow that closes the loop from observed product behavior to code changes.
